description: The figure presents SEM morphology and corresponding EDS elemental distribution maps of a Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C–1.5Be low-density steel strip after rolling at 1050 °C. Panel (a) shows the interfacial junction between austenite and ferrite, highlighting an elongated strip-shaped phase labeled A within a contrasting matrix that contains dispersed particles and pores. Panels (b)–(e) display the spatial distributions of Fe, Mn, C, and Al, respectively, each with a 1 μm scale bar. The Fe map appears relatively uniform across the field, whereas the Mn, C, and Al maps reveal localized contrasts between the elongated strip and the surrounding matrix. The caption reports that the inner strip-shaped structure is Mn-rich and Al-rich and the outer strip-shaped structure is Mn-rich and Al-poor, identifying the elongated phase as ferrite and the surrounding region as austenite. Overall, the figure supports the core conclusion that the rolled 1.5Be steel exhibits a dual-phase microstructure composed of elongated ferrite strips embedded in an austenitic matrix, with characteristic Mn and Al elemental partitioning between the two phases.
